title_en: Ibn Khallikan: Jurist, Judge, and Master Biographer
title_ar: ابن خلكان: الفقيه والقاضي والمؤرخ البارع
content_en:
Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khallikan (608–681 AH / 1211–1282 CE) was a distinguished Shafi'i jurist and judge whose most enduring legacy is his monumental biographical dictionary Wafayat al-A'yan wa-Anba Abna az-Zaman — the Deaths of Eminent Men and the News of the Sons of the Age. Born in Irbil (Erbil, in present-day Iraq) into a scholarly family with roots in the ancient city of Balkh, he received a classical Islamic education in Irbil and traveled widely for further study in Mosul, Aleppo, Cairo, and Damascus.

Ibn Khallikan spent significant periods of his scholarly career in Egypt, where he taught and served in judicial capacities, before being appointed chief judge (qadi al-qudat) of Syria based in Damascus, a position he held on two separate occasions with an intervening period of removal and return. His judicial career brought him into contact with the leading political, intellectual, and cultural figures of the Ayyubid and early Mamluk periods, giving him access to information and networks that enriched his biographical work.

His formation was intellectual as well as legal: he was well versed in Arabic literature, poetry, and the histories of previous Islamic scholars, and his writing style reflects the refined literary culture of the medieval Islamic scholarly class. Unlike some biographical scholars who wrote in a spare technical style, Ibn Khallikan wrote with literary elegance, incorporating poetry and anecdote to illuminate his subjects' characters.

The Wafayat represents decades of scholarly labor. Ibn Khallikan began working on it during his first period in Egypt and continued revising it throughout his later career. The work draws on an extraordinary range of sources — biographical dictionaries, historical chronicles, literary anthologies, and personal inquiry — and reflects Ibn Khallikan's determination to produce a work of both scholarly reliability and literary quality.

His death in Damascus in 681 AH came after a career of tremendous scholarly productivity. The Wafayat stands alone as his great legacy, but contemporaries also valued him as a teacher and legal authority. His students included scholars who carried on the biographical tradition he had elevated, and his influence on the genre of Islamic biography is difficult to overstate.
title_id: Ibn Khallikan: Ahli Hukum, Hakim, dan Ahli Biografi Utama
content_id:
Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khallikan (608–681 H / 1211–1282 M) adalah seorang ahli hukum Syafi'i dan hakim yang terkemuka, warisan abadi terbesarnya adalah kamus biografis monumental Wafayat al-A'yan wa-Anba Abna az-Zaman — Kematian Para Tokoh Terkemuka dan Berita tentang Putra-Putra Zaman. Lahir di Irbil (Erbil, di Irak modern) dari keluarga ulama dengan akar di kota Balkh yang kuno, ia menerima pendidikan Islam klasik di Irbil dan melakukan perjalanan luas untuk studi lebih lanjut di Mosul, Aleppo, Kairo, dan Damaskus.

Ibn Khallikan menghabiskan periode-periode penting karir keilmuannya di Mesir, di mana ia mengajar dan bertugas dalam kapasitas yudisial, sebelum diangkat sebagai hakim kepala (qadhi al-qudhah) Suriah yang berkedudukan di Damaskus, sebuah jabatan yang dipegangnya pada dua kesempatan terpisah dengan periode pencopotan dan pengangkatan kembali di antaranya. Karir yudisialnya membawanya ke dalam kontak dengan tokoh-tokoh politik, intelektual, dan budaya terkemuka dari periode Ayyubiyah dan Mamluk awal, memberinya akses terhadap informasi dan jaringan yang memperkaya karya biografisnya.

Pembentukannya bersifat intelektual sekaligus hukum: ia menguasai sastra Arab, puisi, dan sejarah ulama-ulama Islam sebelumnya, dan gaya tulisannya mencerminkan budaya sastra yang halus dari kelas ulama Islam abad pertengahan. Berbeda dengan beberapa ulama biografi yang menulis dalam gaya teknis yang hemat, Ibn Khallikan menulis dengan keanggunan sastra, menggabungkan puisi dan anekdot untuk menerangi karakter subjek-subjeknya.

Wafayat merupakan hasil kerja keilmuan selama beberapa dekade. Ibn Khallikan mulai mengerjakannya selama periode pertamanya di Mesir dan terus merevisinya sepanjang karir berikutnya. Karya ini mengandalkan sumber-sumber yang luar biasa luas — kamus biografis, kronik historis, antologi sastra, dan penyelidikan pribadi — dan mencerminkan tekad Ibn Khallikan untuk menghasilkan karya yang memiliki keandalan keilmuan sekaligus kualitas sastra.

Kematiannya di Damaskus pada 681 H datang setelah karir produktivitas keilmuan yang luar biasa. Wafayat berdiri sendiri sebagai warisan besarnya, tetapi para sezamannya juga menghargainya sebagai guru dan otoritas hukum. Murid-muridnya mencakup ulama-ulama yang melanjutkan tradisi biografi yang ia tinggikan, dan pengaruhnya terhadap genre biografi Islam sangat sulit dilebih-lebihkan.
